message:
feat(fcp-core): migrate capability IDs to Arc<str> and expand capability system

Migrates ConnectorId, InstanceId, and OperationId internal storage from
String to Arc<str> to reduce clone costs in high-frequency capability
lookups across the connector mesh, where IDs are shared across many
data structures simultaneously.

Also expands the capability system with comprehensive capability usage
tracking, aggregation, reporting, and suggestion infrastructure. Adds
capabilities dispatch to fwc CLI with report/suggest/export subcommands
supporting zone and connector filtering, host-backed or manifest-backed
metadata resolution, and structured JSON output.

Updates .beads/issues.jsonl with latest bead tracking notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","title":"[FWC] History UX: pending approvals, blocked actions, and resumable work queue","description":"# [FWC] History UX: pending approvals, blocked actions, and resumable work queue

## Goal
Give agents and operators one explicit place to inspect work that is waiting on approval, blocked by an earlier preflight, or no longer runnable because its approval context expired or changed.

## Scope
- list and filter pending, approved, denied, expired, superseded, and resumed approval-backed actions by connector, operation, zone, profile, risk, and age
- show the origin preflight, macro, or pipeline stage, linked receipts or handles, and the exact resume or abandon command
- compact blocked-work summaries with drill-down paths rather than forcing users to reconstruct state from scattered receipts
- clear queue semantics for both primitive actions and higher-level workflow actions

## Acceptance criteria
- a caller can answer \"what is waiting, what expired, and how do I continue or discard it?\" from `fwc` alone
- approval-backed work no longer disappears into hidden preflight state or half-remembered receipts","notes":"Treat this as the explicit answer to approval-state drift. It should unify primitive and workflow actions, expose expiry or supersession clearly, and make resume or abandon commands obvious. Cover it with fast snapshots, host-backed integration, confusion-corpus cases, operator and agent E2E playbooks, and debugging docs with detailed structured logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:51.222623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.620836Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["approvals","cli","fwc","history"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.187154Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.22.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.401478Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:51.222623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.620801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","title":"[FWC] Host-first truthful runtime migration: no silent fallbacks, no fake auth, no simulated control paths","description":"# [FWC] Host-first truthful runtime migration: no silent fallbacks, no fake auth, no simulated control paths

## Goal
Make `fwc` the canonical agent-native shell over live `fcp-host` truth for runtime control, while keeping offline file/package/policy workflows explicit and honest.

## Why this branch exists
The current `fwc` implementation already has strong agent ergonomics, but it still mixes live host-backed behavior with workspace-manifest fallbacks, placeholder auth paths, assumed simulation support, and a few planning-only runtime verbs. That combination is good enough for demos and partial workflows, but it is not yet the "real control plane for all of FCP" standard we want.

## Problem statement
Today an agent can sometimes believe it is operating the live system when it is actually reading static manifests, mutating a local file that still requires restart, using placeholder capability tokens, or seeing metadata that was guessed by the host instead of truthfully reported by the connector/runtime.

This branch exists to remove that ambiguity.

## Scope
- define the hard runtime boundary between live host truth and explicit offline artifact work
- complete the host admin surface for lifecycle/config/install/reload/logs/events/mesh/registry control
- replace fake auth/approval paths with real capability issuance, holder proof, and verification
- add a real host-routed simulate path and stop advertising support by assumption
- make search/template/validate/export-tools/serve-mcp host-authoritative for live runtime usage
- remove synthetic metadata defaults so unknown remains unknown
- make workflow/intent layers refuse or clearly label any runtime verb that is not actually backed by the host
- prove the result through host-backed integration, E2E evidence, and docs/playbooks","design":"Runtime commands must be host-first and fail closed when live truth is unavailable. Offline workflows remain supported, but they must be explicit in naming, help text, and output envelopes. Unsupported capabilities are surfaced as unsupported or unknown, never emulated with placeholder success.","acceptance_criteria":"No live runtime command silently falls back from host state to workspace manifests or local files.
No non-test runtime path uses test tokens, empty approval placeholders, or assumed capability support.
Simulate is either real and bounded or reported as unsupported.
Host and CLI metadata never invent archetypes, rate limits, or capability flags when the runtime does not know them.
Planning-only workflow verbs do not masquerade as canonical runtime control paths.
Integration, E2E, and docs make the truthful model obvious to future operators and agents.","notes":"This branch complements the existing `fwc` epic and active parity work by making the no-fakes closure criteria explicit and executable inside the beads graph.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:24:48.166767Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:26:20.834459Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","host-first","planning","runtime","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:24:48.166767Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.1","title":"[FWC] Truthful runtime boundary: host-authoritative live commands and explicit offline artifact mode","description":"Define and enforce the boundary between live runtime truth and offline artifact work.

This track exists because `fwc` currently has flows where a host-aware command can silently degrade into workspace-manifest or local-file behavior. That ambiguity trains agents to trust stale or partial state. The end state is that every command clearly declares whether it is talking to a live host, operating on offline artifacts, or supporting both through an explicit mode switch.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:26:21.211362Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:26:21.211362Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","mode","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:26:21.211362Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.2","title":"[FWC] Truthful host admin surface: live config, lifecycle, install, reload, logs, events, mesh, and registry control","description":"Complete the live `fcp-host` admin/control surface that `fwc` needs in order to manage the real system rather than local files and restart instructions.

This track covers config mutation, lifecycle transitions, package install/update/uninstall/reload control, logs/events/receipts, and host-visible mesh/registry administration. It is the substrate that makes the other truthfulness requirements possible.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:05.502854Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:05.502854Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fwc","host","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:05.502854Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.3","title":"[FWC] Truthful auth path: real capability issuance, holder proofs, approvals, and runtime enforcement","description":"Replace placeholder runtime auth semantics with real capability issuance, holder proof transport, approval token handling, and host-side verification.

A truthful control plane cannot keep shipping test tokens or empty approval placeholders through live request builders. This track closes that gap all the way from CLI acquisition and transport to host enforcement and denial/approval evidence.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:06.142160Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:06.142160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","fwc","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:06.142160Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.4","title":"[FWC] Truthful simulate path: real host-routed simulation and honest capability advertisement","description":"Turn `simulate` into a real bounded host-routed connector capability instead of a renamed preflight path or an assumed feature bit.

This track owns both the runtime path and the capability advertisement contract so the CLI only claims simulation support where the connector and host can actually provide truthful bounded results.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:06.778900Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:06.778900Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","simulate","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:06.778900Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.5","title":"[FWC] Truthful catalog surfaces: host-authoritative search, templates, validation, export-tools, and serve-mcp","description":"Make the agent-facing catalog surfaces truthful by routing live runtime discovery, templating, validation, tool export, and MCP serving through host introspection rather than workspace guesses.

This is the track that removes the split brain where `fwc` feels highly ergonomic but may still be showing the wrong inventory for the actual running system.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.426154Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.426154Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["catalog","fwc","mcp","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.426154Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.6","title":"[FWC] Truthful metadata contract: unknown stays unknown and nothing gets invented","description":"Remove any invented runtime metadata from host and CLI surfaces.

If archetype, rate limits, event support, auth requirements, or related capability descriptors are not known truthfully, the system must say so instead of manufacturing a plausible default. The goal is to make incorrect confidence impossible.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.894799Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.894799Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","metadata","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.894799Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.7","title":"[FWC] Truthful workflow execution: no planning-only runtime verbs in the canonical control path","description":"Clean up the workflow and intent layers so the canonical agent path never implies that planning-only or stubbed runtime control exists when it does not.

This track owns `pipe`, `recipe`, `pipeline`, and intent-derived lifecycle/control verbs that must either execute via real host primitives or fail honestly with explicit unsupported, offline, or planned semantics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:08.618939Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:08.618939Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","truthfulness","workflow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:08.618939Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.8","title":"[FWC] Truthfulness verification and operator guidance: integration, E2E, docs, and migration","description":"Prove and teach the host-first truthful model through integration coverage, end-to-end evidence, and docs/playbooks.

This track exists because a control plane can look truthful in one narrow command path and still regress silently elsewhere unless the invariants are encoded in tests, scenario evidence, and operator guidance.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:09.209684Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:09.209684Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fwc","test","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:09.209684Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","title":"[FWC] Connector/host readiness contract for discovery, config, lifecycle, and invocation","description":"# [FWC] Connector/host readiness contract for discovery, config, lifecycle, and invocation

## Goal
Define the minimum metadata and RPC surface that a connector and `fcp-host` must expose in order for `fwc` to support it cleanly.

## Why this exists
The current workspace has real connector crates, but metadata quality is uneven: some areas are richly introspectable while others still rely on simulated CLI output or connector-local conventions. `fwc` needs a strict readiness contract so we know what must be standardized instead of hand-waving around gaps.

## The contract should cover
### Host-visible connector summary
- canonical connector id, display name, version, categories/archetypes
- enabled/disabled state and health state
- installation/pinning status
- capability/risk summary and operation counts

### Operation metadata
- operation id and human summary
- input schema and output schema
- capability requirement, risk level, safety tier, idempotency class
- approval requirements and simulate support
- examples/agent hints for the happy path and common failure modes

### Config metadata
- config schema with secrets clearly marked/redacted
- default values, required fields, and secretless alternatives (`credential_id` paths)
- doctor/self-check expectations after configure
- import/export representation rules

### Lifecycle/state metadata
- whether the connector supports streaming/events
- whether it is configured, handshaken, running, degraded, or blocked
- what host actions are legal from the current state

## Deliverables
- A formal readiness checklist for connectors and host endpoints.
- Canonical payload shapes for discovery/show/config/invoke/lifecycle flows.
- A list of mandatory fields that `fwc` can rely on without connector-specific hacks.
- Explicit gap categories so later cohort beads can remediate missing coverage systematically.

## Acceptance criteria
- We can look at any connector and determine objectively whether it is `fwc`-ready.
- The contract is strong enough to drive generated help/examples and dynamic command aliasing.
- The contract avoids manual per-connector shims except where a justified compatibility layer is intentionally specified.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.627638Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","updated_at":"2026-03-08T01:31:28.535895Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T01:31:14.903224Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fwc","host","schemas"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.627638Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:24.629879Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:25.276224Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1039,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the connector/host readiness contract as crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs. Deliverables: (1) Formal readiness checklist — ReadinessVerdict with SummaryReadiness, OperationsReadiness, ConfigReadiness, LifecycleReadiness areas, (2) Canonical payload shapes — ConnectorSummary, ConnectorDetail, OperationSummary, HealthSummary, RateLimitSummary for discovery/show/config/invoke/lifecycle, (3) Mandatory field constants — MANDATORY_SUMMARY_FIELDS, MANDATORY_OPERATION_FIELDS, RECOMMENDED_OPERATION_FIELDS, (4) Gap categories — GapCategory enum (Identity, OperationMetadata, ConfigSchema, Lifecycle, AgentHints, EventSupport, RateLimits, ApprovalPolicy) with GapSeverity (Blocking, Degraded, Cosmetic), (5) ConnectorCohort enum for grouping remediation work, (6) evaluate_introspection() function that produces ReadinessVerdict from raw introspection JSON, (7) 23 unit tests covering serialization round-trips, evaluation logic, edge cases, severity ordering. Total fwc tests: 245, clippy clean. — SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:21:09Z"},{"id":1042,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Verified complete: readiness.rs (2,720 lines, 88 tests) implements full readiness contract. ReadinessVerdict (Ready/PartiallyReady/NotReady) with per-area checklists (Summary, Operations, Config, Lifecycle). Mandatory fields checklist for all connectors. Gap categories for systematic cohort remediation. Covers canonical_id, display_name, archetypes, semver, description, operation_count, risk_summary, schema availability, secret redaction, enable/disable support. All 454 fwc tests pass, clippy clean. — SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:31:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","title":"[FWC] Host admin API for connector inventory, lifecycle, config, invoke, and audit-backed state changes","description":"# [FWC] Host admin API for connector inventory, lifecycle, config, invoke, and audit-backed state changes

## Goal
Extend `fcp-host` with the concrete management/API surface that `fwc` needs so the standalone CLI can operate on real installed connectors rather than simulated data.

## Background
`fcp-host` already owns orchestration and discovery is under active work, but `fwc` needs a broader admin plane: lifecycle transitions, config reads/writes, invoke/simulate bridges, status inspection, and audit-friendly state changes.

## Scope
- Inventory/search/show endpoints for connectors and operations.
- Lifecycle endpoints for install/verify/enable/disable/start/stop/restart/update/pin/unpin/status.
- Config endpoints for schema/get/set/unset/import/export/validate/doctor.
- Invoke/simulate/batch/stream/log-tail endpoints or equivalent RPC methods.
- Audit/event semantics for state-changing admin actions.

## Critical constraint
This bead must eliminate simulated/demo connector CLI data as a required dependency for `fwc`. The host is the runtime source of truth.

## Deliverables
- Endpoint/RPC definitions with stable request/response payloads.
- Permission/policy model for admin actions vs connector operations.
- Deterministic error codes and reason payloads for all admin actions.
- Structured logging and audit emission rules for config/lifecycle changes.
- Test harness hooks so the later `fwc` integration beads can exercise real host flows.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fwc` can rely on host APIs instead of duplicating connector-local logic.
- State changes are auditable and recoverable.
- The management surface is sufficiently complete that `fwc` does not need direct filesystem or process hacks to operate connectors.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.758334Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:28.510280Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fwc","host","management"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.758334Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:26.680728Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:27.029763Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2h7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:28.100678Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:27.555521Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-y1q8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:28.510186Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","title":"[FWC] Host admin API: inventory and operation introspection RPC contract","description":"# [FWC] Host admin API: inventory and operation introspection RPC contract

## Goal
Define the host-backed inventory and operation-descriptor payloads that make every connector and every operation discoverable from `fcp-host` without `fwc` maintaining bespoke command tables.

## Scope
- connector inventory fields for provider, cohort, archetype, risk, capabilities, health, install state, and availability
- operation descriptors for input schema, output schema, examples, approvals, streaming/log support, and idempotency hints
- filter/search/pagination/cache semantics so discovery stays token-efficient at workspace scale
- explicit representation of metadata gaps so the CLI can surface missing readiness cleanly instead of guessing

## Deliverables
- stable request/response contract for inventory, show, ops, schema, and example reads
- canonical field glossary shared by CLI rendering, completions, workflow planning, and docs

## Acceptance criteria
- `fwc list/show/ops/schema/examples` can be driven from host inventory alone
- missing or partial connector metadata becomes explicit and testable rather than silently papered over","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzePrairie","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T00:01:55.575110Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T00:01:55.575096Z","close_reason":"Completed: fwc host-backed list/show/ops/schema/examples wired to fcp-host inventory/introspection RPCs with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fwc","host","introspection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2h7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1505,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","author":"BronzePrairie","text":"Implemented host-backed fwc list/show/ops/schema/examples --host <endpoint> using fcp-host /rpc/discover, /rpc/connectors/{id}, and /rpc/introspect/{id} while preserving manifest-backed defaults when --host is absent. Added host-path selector resolution, host metadata gap reporting, and focused mock-host tests for list/show/ops/schema/examples. Verification so far: targeted rch cargo check for fwc passed; targeted host-path fwc tests passed; cargo clippy -p fwc --all-targets -- -D warnings is currently blocked by pre-existing errors in crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs; workspace cargo check --workspace --all-targets is blocked by pre-existing fcp-registry errors converting fcp_cbor::SerializationError into RegistryError.","created_at":"2026-03-10T23:59:07Z"}]}
